title: Registry Identity, SBOM, Provenance, Attestations, and Promotion · Release Engineering Workshop
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working attested image release record for registry identity, sbom, provenance, attestations, and promotion. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of attested image release record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Generate human-readable tags while binding promotion, deployment, rollback, and evidence to one registry digest.
Generate image SBOM and max-appropriate provenance, identify builder, source, inputs, subject digest, limits, and registry attachment.
Use actions/attest with least permissions and exact subject path or digest while recognizing plan, repository visibility, and registry constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-slsa-level-claim-boundary-1y80y7". Tailor its attested-release.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary, keep Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-slsa-level-claim-boundary-1y80y7
  - competency: ci-slsa-level-claim-boundary
    artifact: attested-release.yaml
    decision: slsa level claim boundary
    environment: incident replay using redacted run metadata and immutable artifacts
    fault-injection: change an action tag to a different commit without workflow edits
    retained-proof: runner image runtime version authority network and teardown record
    case-key: 1y80y7
    change: |
      subject: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      tags: [v2.4.0, stable]
      sbom: spdx-attached
      provenance: mode-max
      builder: github-hosted-reviewed-workflow
      actions-attest: 4
      attestation-subject: exact-digest
      verification: issuer-repository-workflow-builder-subject
      promotion: same-digest
      rollback: prior-verified-digest
    verify: verify image digest SBOM provenance signature issuer repository workflow builder subject predicate policy and promotion identity
    failure: Reject tags as identity, detached unbound SBOM, build-arg secrets, signature-only trust, unverified attestations, or transitive SLSA claims.
    repair: Bind metadata to digest, generate safe attestations, verify identity and expectations downstream, promote once, and state SLSA limits.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop candidate is promoted by mutable tag with partial SBOM and provenance, an unverified attestation, and policy exception 2. Bind image index, platforms, signatures, attestations, verification policy, and exception expiry. Competency probe: Map actual build and source controls to SLSA 1.2 tracks and levels without claiming transitive dependency or code-quality guarantees.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary, keep Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map actual build and source controls to SLSA 1.2 tracks and levels without claiming transitive dependency or code-quality guarantees.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop candidate is promoted by mutable tag with partial SBOM and provenance, an unverified attestation, and policy exception 2. Bind image index, platforms, signatures, attestations, verification policy, and exception expiry. Competency probe: Map actual build and source controls to SLSA 1.2 tracks and levels without claiming transitive dependency or code-quality guarantees.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Slsa Level Claim Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: SLSA Build Level 3 means all dependencies are secure and the software is vulnerability-free.
